metadata.title:
FLYNN TRIAL


BEGINS IN


THE COURT
MOTION FOR CHANGE OF
vENUEE BECAUSE OF


PREJUDICE DENIED.
HINDLEY'S AFFIDAVIT
QUESTIONS AS TO THEIR PRE-.
JUDGES TOWARD WON.


ANS CAUSE BARRED.

HINDLEY'S AFFIDAVIT

pages.raw_text:
. With a refusal to grant change
OF venue, asked for on the ground
OF alleged prejudice, the trial Of
Elizabeth Gurley Flynn, the young
woman leader Of the Industrial
Workers of the World, arrested for
alleged conspiracy in connection
with the street speaking fight, was
begun before Judge Henry L. Ken
nan and jury in the criminal de-
partment of the superior court this
morning.


Only four veniremen were exam
med this morning and two of these
were excused. 1t will take all day
to get jury and probably a week
to try the case.


A spirited fight ensued over the
effort to secure change of venue
by Miss Flynn. She alleged that
because of hostile police action and
newspaper criticism a public preju-
dice existed here, and for this rea-
son she wanted the case sent to a
court where these conditions were
not to be found.


The state denied the existence of
this prejudice, and for proof, among
other things presented an affidavit
from the Rev. w. J. Hindley to the
effect that he thought no prejudice
existed and that the young woman
defendant could get a square deal.
In opposition to this affidavit of
Rev. Hindley it was shown that he
gave out an interview only few
days ago in which he denounced
the l. w. w. free speech fight and
praised the police as the champions
of all that was good and holy.
In the examination of the venire.
men all reference to their views or
prejudices toward socialism or in
| defense wanted to go into this in
order to bar from jury duty men
that might be prejudiced against
the defendant because she was en-
gagged in this work.


| The courtroom was crowded dur
ing the hearing this morning and a
record-breaking attendance is look
ed for all week. Prosecutor Pugh
is in personal charge of the case
and Fred Moore and Thomas j..
Symmes of Chicago are looking
after the interests of Miss Flynn.


81 am not afraid" said Miss
Flynn after the morning session
closed. Il am ready to go to prison
if the court DEGREES that l have
committed conspiracy. | think
can establish my innocence of this
charge to the satisfaction of all fair
minded men,"
